items.title: Training vs inference lifecycle
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  labeled["labeled data (x, y)"]
  trainPhase["training: update theta"]
  frozen["frozen model f_theta"]
  newX["new input x"]
  inferPhase["inference: y_hat = f_theta(x)"]
  labeled --> trainPhase --> frozen
  newX --> inferPhase
  frozen --> inferPhase
items.caption: Training consumes labels and changes θ; inference applies the frozen model to unlabeled x.

items.title: Training step internals
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  x["input x"]
  y["label y"]
  fwd["forward: y_hat = f_theta(x)"]
  lossNode["loss L(y_hat, y)"]
  bwd["backward: grad theta"]
  upd["update theta"]
  x --> fwd
  fwd --> lossNode
  y --> lossNode
  lossNode --> bwd --> upd
items.caption: Each training iteration needs both x and y; gradients update parameters.

items.title: Inference step (no labels)
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  xNew["new x"]
  fwdOnly["forward pass only"]
  yHat["prediction y_hat"]
  xNew --> fwdOnly --> yHat
items.caption: Inference is a single forward pass with fixed θ — no loss, no backward, no label y.

items.title: fit and predict API mapping
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  fit["fit(X, y)"]
  trainBox["training phase"]
  predict["predict(X_new)"]
  inferBox["inference phase"]
  fit --> trainBox
  predict --> inferBox
items.caption: Library APIs name the two phases: fit learns from labels; predict serves new inputs.
